draft.title: a coffee shop landing page
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a coffee shop landing page

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer intimate, organic warmth over sleek digital minimalism for coffee shop branding
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool, organic vs digital
facade.label: Steaming cup, dark wood
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into natural textures, steam/warmth imagery, wooden furniture photography, earthy color palette, handcrafted aesthetic
facade.rejectImplies: Explore cooler tones, modern materials, minimalist layouts, bright whites/grays, tech-forward interface elements

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer intimate, organic warmth over sleek digital minimalism for coffee shop branding
evidence.implication: Lean into natural textures, steam/warmth imagery, wooden furniture photography, earthy color palette, handcrafted aesthetic

facade.hypothesis: Coffee shop landing pages perform better when showing metrics (price, rating, hours) upfront rather than storytelling copy
facade.label: Numbers over narrative
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers quantitative, scannable information design for this coffee shop site
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers qualitative, story-driven copy and descriptive language over hard data

facade.hypothesis: Coffee shops benefit from natural material aesthetics that feel inviting and craft-oriented rather than sterile or minimal
facade.label: Warm wood tones
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward earthy, natural color schemes with browns, tans, and organic textures — likely wants a rustic or artisanal vibe
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers cooler palettes, modern minimalism, or non-wood aesthetics — may want sleek, contemporary, or bright/airy feel

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a friendly, approachable tone over corporate formality for a neighborhood coffee shop
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and personality — friendly vs professional
facade.label: Casual & conversational
facade.acceptImplies: Landing page should use warm, relatable language; first-person voice; playful tone; colloquial phrasing like 'our favorite espresso' instead of 'premium arabica selections'
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer a more polished, professional tone; measured language; third-person perspective; formal product descriptions; positioning as upscale or expertise-driven rather than community-focused

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer navigation anchored to the left while hero imagery dominates the right, creating clear information hierarchy and reducing cognitive load on entry.
facade.label: Sidebar with hero
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into fixed left nav; hero becomes dominant visual anchor; likely signals preference for persistent wayfinding and asymmetric layouts
facade.rejectImplies: User may favor centered, full-width storytelling; top navigation preferred; more symmetrical or card-based entry experience

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer gradual content discovery through scroll-triggered animations over instant page loads
facade.axisTargeted: Transition timing: progressive reveal vs. all-at-once
facade.label: Smooth scroll to reveal
facade.acceptImplies: User values animated entrance effects and staggered content animations for engagement and storytelling
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers instant, snappy interactions where all content loads immediately without animation delays

axes.label: Whether the user wants a warm, inviting visual aesthetic (earthy tones, natural materials) vs. a clean, modern minimalist look for their coffee shop site
Whether the user prefers a traditional vertical menu layout with clear sections vs. an immersive full-screen hero experience that dominates the landing
Whether the user wants a professional, knowledgeable tone about coffee craft vs. a casual, playful personality that emphasizes community and fun
Whether the user envisions a calm, peaceful browsing experience vs. an energetic, vibrant atmosphere that feels bustling and alive
Whether the user wants prominent display of menu items and pricing upfront vs. storytelling and atmosphere that gradually reveals practical details
Whether the user prefers smooth, subtle animations that feel sophisticated vs. playful, noticeable interactions that draw attention and delight

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether the user wants a warm, inviting visual aesthetic (earthy tones, natural materials) vs. a clean, modern minimalist look for their coffee shop site
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

facade.hypothesis: Users want clean, uncluttered visual hierarchy over cozy maximalism in coffee shop landing design
facade.label: Minimalist counter
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize whitespace, typography-forward layout, sleek materials (steel/glass), modern sans-serif, sparse product showcase
facade.rejectImplies: User confirms preference for layered textures, warm clutter, handmade elements, lived-in atmosphere—doubling down on organic warmth signal

draft.title: Brew Haven Coffee Shop
draft.summary: A warm, inviting landing page for a specialty coffee shop featuring hero section, featured drinks, and location details with earthy, coffee-inspired color palette.
draft.html:
Brew Haven

Specialty coffee, crafted daily

Freshly roasted beans, perfectly brewed

Featured Drinks

Single Origin Espresso
Ethiopian Yirgacheffe with notes of blueberry and jasmine
4.50
Honey Oat Latte
Smooth espresso with steamed oat milk and local honey
5.25
Cold Brew Concentrate
12-hour steeped, served over ice with your choice of milk
4.75

Visit Us

Address 427 Oak Street, Downtown
Hours Mon–Fri: 7am–6pm, Sat–Sun: 8am–5pm
Phone (555) 234-5678
© 2024 Brew Haven. All rights reserved.
